title: SIGSEGV in EarlyPerfInliner (isCallerAndCalleeLayoutConstraintsCompatible) on isolated deinit of generic class at -O — 3-line repro
author: pixelmatrix
body:
### Description

`swift-frontend` crashes with a segmentation fault (null dereference at `0x8`) in `isCallerAndCalleeLayoutConstraintsCompatible(swift::FullApplySite)` while running `EarlyPerfInliner` on the **isolated deinit of a generic class**, when compiling with `-O` or `-Osize` for a deployment target that requires the isolated-deinit back-deployment path.

The reproduction is 3 lines and 100% deterministic — no minimum module size or other code required.

### Reproduction

```swift
@MainActor final class C<T> {
  @MainActor deinit {}
}
```

```
% xcrun -sdk iphoneos swiftc -O -target arm64-apple-ios17.0 -c repro.swift -o /dev/null
```

Crashes with:

```
1.	Apple Swift version 6.3.2 (swiftlang-6.3.2.1.108 clang-2100.1.1.101)
2.	Compiling with effective version 5.10
3.	While evaluating request ExecuteSILPipelineRequest(Run pipelines { PrepareOptimizationPasses, EarlyModulePasses, HighLevel,Function+EarlyLoopOpt, HighLevel,Module+StackPromote, MidLevel,Function, ClosureSpecialize, LowLevel,Function, LateLoopOpt, SIL Debug Info Generator } on SIL for repro)
4.	While running pass #451 SILFunctionTransform "EarlyPerfInliner" on SILFunction "@$s5repro1CCfD".
...
4  swift-frontend  isCallerAndCalleeLayoutConstraintsCompatible(swift::FullApplySite) + 236
5  swift-frontend  isCallerAndCalleeLayoutConstraintsCompatible(swift::FullApplySite) + 236
6  swift-frontend  swift::getEligibleFunction(swift::FullApplySite, swift::InlineSelection, swift::IsSelfRecursiveAnalysis*) + 1988
7  swift-frontend  (anonymous namespace)::SILPerformanceInliner::collectAppliesToInline(...)
```

### Matrix (Apple Swift 6.3.2 / swiftlang-6.3.2.1.108, Xcode 26.5)

All four conditions are required:

| Condition | Notes |
|---|---|
| Generic class | non-generic `C` with isolated deinit compiles fine |
| Isolated deinit (`@MainActor deinit`, SE-0371) | plain `deinit` compiles fine |
| `-O` or `-Osize` | `-Onone` fine (only optimized/archive builds affected) |
| Deployment target predating native isolated-deinit runtime support | `arm64-apple-ios17.0`/`18.0` and `arm64-apple-macos13.0`/`15.0` crash; `arm64-apple-ios26.0` does not |

The deployment-target dependence indicates the trigger is the **back-deployment lowering of isolated deinit**: the deallocating deinit `$s...CfD` contains an apply of the back-deploy shim that carries the class's substitution map while the callee's lowered function type has **no invocation generic signature**. `getEligibleFunction` guards the call to `isCallerAndCalleeLayoutConstraintsCompatible` only with `AI.hasSubstitutions()`, and that function then does:

```cpp
auto CalleeSig = Callee->getLoweredFunctionType()->getInvocationGenericSignature();
...
CalleeSig->forEachParam(...)   // CalleeSig is null → SIGSEGV at offset 0x8
```

(`lib/SILOptimizer/Utils/PerformanceInlinerUtils.cpp`)

Also reproduces with Apple Swift 6.2.4 (Xcode 26.3, seen in CI archives). Does **not** reproduce with the Xcode 27.0 beta toolchain (swiftc from Build 27A5209h), so main appears fixed — filing this for a potential 6.3.x backport, since every optimized build of a generic class with an isolated deinit targeting currently-supported OS versions crashes the 26.x compilers.

### Workarounds

- `@_optimize(none)` on the deinit
- Removing the isolation from the deinit (plain `deinit`)
- Raising the deployment target to the OS 26 runtimes

### Related

Same crash site, different triggers: #88173 (`-default-isolation MainActor`), #90150 / #89851 (synthesized deinit of generic `UIViewRepresentable.Coordinator`).

### Environment

- Apple Swift version 6.3.2 (swiftlang-6.3.2.1.108 clang-2100.1.1.101), Xcode 26.5 (also 6.2.4 / Xcode 26.3)
- macOS 15.6 / 26.x, Apple silicon

title: Type checker performance: Data.+ overloads vs. Data.init(base64Encoded:)
author: nevil
body:
### Description

In one very specific case the 6.4 compiler generates the "the compiler is unable to type-check this expression in reasonable time; try breaking up the expression into distinct sub-expressions" where previous versions did not.

Though it is not a very severe regression.


### Reproduction

```sh
swiftc bug.swift
```

```swift
import Foundation

let a = Data(base64Encoded: "" +
    "1" +
    "2" +
    "3" +
    "4" +
    "5" +
    "6", options: [])
```

```text
6.4-bug.swift:19:9: error: the compiler is unable to type-check this expression in reasonable time; try breaking up the expression into distinct sub-expressions
17 | */
18 |
19 | let a = Data(base64Encoded: "" +
   |         `- error: the compiler is unable to type-check this expression in reasonable time; try breaking up the expression into distinct sub-expressions
20 |     "1" +
21 |     "2" +
```

### Expected behavior

It compiles without error.


### Environment

Apple Swift version 6.4-dev (LLVM fe909703d5034b3, Swift 35c7b288937df5b)
Target: arm64-apple-macosx26.0
Build config: +assertions

### Additional information

_No response_

title: Compiler crashes when catching a typed throw
author: BigZaphod
body:
### Description

Hit a crash while attempting to update some code to use typed throws. This took awhile to track down to a minimum reproduction. Initially hit this using Swift 6.3.2 (Xcode 26.5), but then decided to check the latest Xcode 27 beta and whichever version of Swift came with that and eventually got a minimum reproduction using Swift 6.4.

It seems to be caused when you have a `catch` that casts `error` to the one type of `Error` that the throwing function can throw. So the workaround is easy enough - just remove the `let e as MyError` in the code below.

But when upgrading a bunch of old code to typed throws, this was crashing a lot which makes migration of old code to the new safer code a bit annoying and difficult.

### Reproduction

```swift
struct MyError: Error { let message: String }
func mightFail() throws(MyError) { throw MyError(message: "boom") }
func run() throws { do { try mightFail() } catch let e as MyError { _ = e.message } }
```

title: Split Backtraing Tests into friendly vs non-friendly test files and mark only friendly tests unsupported under remote-run
author: duow310
body:
### Description

This is a follow-up for https://github.com/swiftlang/swift/pull/90512. `preset=friendly` tests are not currently supported under remote-run. The PR therefore marked them as unsupported. Since the tests currently mix friendly and non-friendly checks, we lose coverage for non-friendly checks this way. Splitting the tests into friendly and non-friendly files and only marking the friendly tests unsupported gives us back that coverage.

### Additional information

_No response_

title: Migrate Foundation to `CopyFile2` from `CopyFile`
author: compnerd
body:
### Description

swift-foundation's FileManager migrated over the `CopyFile` operation instead of updating to `CopyFile2` which gives a small behavioural improvement on ReFS. We should update that to get the best performance.

title: Impossible to extend macro-generated type with public memberwise initializer
author: stephencelis
body:
### Description

An existing diagnostic prevents you from defining a public memberwise initializer for a type in an extension:

> invalid redeclaration of synthesized memberwise 'init'

…but this is the only reasonable way to do so for a type generated by a macro.

Six years ago a suggestion was made on the forums that same-file extensions should be able to define memberwise initializers: https://forums.swift.org/t/why-doesn-t-an-init-in-an-extension-suppress-the-synthesized-init/35182/ It didn't get traction but it also didn't have pushback.

This limitation seems more relevant than ever with macros now in the language.

### Reproduction

```swift
public struct A {
}

extension A {
  public init() {}  // 🛑
}
```


### Expected behavior

I expect a same-file extension to be able to define the memberwise initializer publicly.

### Environment

swift-driver version: 1.168.4 Apple Swift version 6.4 (swiftlang-6.4.0.25.4 clang-2100.3.25.1)
Target: arm64-apple-macosx26.0

### Additional information

_No response_

title: Convert installer to multi-container bundles
author: compnerd
body:
### Description

The windows installer is growing, and we need to convert it to multi-container bundles.

### Additional information

_No response_
